project_summary: Heuristic proposition fallback generated from evidence units.

propositions.text: It should be able to handle different types of products and quantities.
Yoggie should work closely with our organization on future enhancements and new features.
The system must exhibit specific functionalities under certain conditions, which is important for us because it ensures that all the necessary features are covered.
We need the system to send emails to customers and vendors as quickly as possible — ideally in less than a second.
For both customers and vendors, these emails should be triggered under specific conditions such as order confirmations, payment reminders, account updates, and support requests.
For our e-commerce platform, it should be able to process transactions quickly — ideally within less than 2 seconds from when a customer clicks 'buy now'.
We've had issues with slow login times, so we need the system to authenticate users efficiently, ensuring that it never takes more than 5 seconds for a user to log in.
For returning customers, the system should remember their card details but verify the card is still valid upon each login.
Additionally, the system must support multiple browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge on both Windows and macOS operating systems.
The user interface should display product titles and metadata prominently but never show any private information like credit card numbers or passwords in the browser interface.
Only authorized users, identified by their unique login credentials, should have access to certain administrative features.
We must not allow unauthorized users to modify or view sensitive data.
Lastly, we need robust logging and auditing capabilities for all user activities, ensuring that every action is recorded and can be traced back to a specific user.
Additionally, the system should automatically detect and block IP addresses that show signs of DOS attacks.
if someone tries to log in multiple times without success, it should lock them out after a certain number of failed attempts.
This validation should occur every time a new email address is entered or an existing one is updated.
The system must use a SQL-based database for both inventory and other data storage to ensure consistency and reliability.
For customer emails, we need the system to send an email confirmation that includes the items ordered and their tracking number.
This should happen automatically after the order is placed.
The web interface must be consistent across different browsers like Chrome, Firefox, and Safari, ensuring a user-friendly experience for our customers.
For customer support queries, the system should send an email to our System Administrator whenever a technical issue is raised by customers or sales staff.
The content of these emails should include details like the user's contact information and the nature of the problem so that it can be addressed promptly.
This email should contain the tracking number for easy reference.
Customers must access the web order system through a secure HTTPS connection from any compatible browser.
We need the system to send an email confirmation to customers as soon as their order is placed.
This email should include details about the items they ordered and the shipping address, along with a tracking number for easy reference.
It's important because it helps ensure that customers are aware of their order status and can track their deliveries.
During peak usage times, such as when we have a large number of concurrent users, the system should be able to handle searches efficiently.
For example, it should use Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm for searching, and it needs to perform these searches quickly — ideally in less than 1 second per search.
Specifically, it should be able to restore 1000 records per second and retrieve up to 200 products per second without any significant slowdowns.
Ideally it would happen automatically at least once a day, but we should also have the option to manually trigger these tests whenever necessary.
For sure, we need the system to send out automated test emails during scheduled maintenance and after software updates.
During maintenance windows, say between 8 PM and 2 AM on Tuesday nights, the system should automatically check if all outgoing emails are working properly.
Similarly, right after a new version of the software is deployed, we want to make sure everything is still functioning as expected, so the system should send out test emails then too.
This way, any issues can be caught early and addressed before they affect our users.
Specifically, the system should start backing up critical data between 2 AM and 6 AM local time.
Additionally, before performing maintenance tasks such as software updates or server checks, it should complete a backup to ensure that any changes are safe and can be reverted if necessary.
Sales people need to be able to place orders and manage their own profiles within the Weborder System.
Their accounts must already be registered before they can log in.
Once logged in, sales people should have access to order management tools but never administrative functions that only an Administrator would handle.
Customer accounts also need to be registered for them to use the system, but customers should not have the ability to manage orders or other sensitive information.
Customers should be able to add items to their cart and view product details.
When they log in, the system should allow them to access their order history but never let them modify or delete orders that aren't theirs.
It's important because we want to protect user data and ensure only authorized users can make changes.
Additionally, before performing any maintenance tasks, such as software updates or server checks, the system should automatically back up all critical data.
This way, if something goes wrong with our primary storage, we can quickly recover from the last backup without losing too much information.
It should be easy for us to migrate or back up via another USB drive as per AUTO-0035.
For our system to operate smoothly, we need a fixed hardware setup that can be updated via an internet connection.
It's crucial for us to have reliable network connectivity so that we can patch and maintain the system as needed without downtime.
